zh.lifeSummary: Lucia De Ross於1270年出生在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾普倫普頓。她於大約1295年與Robert De Plumpton III在英國英格蘭越可爾普倫普頓 白 艾可萊薩爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1332年在英國英格蘭越可爾普倫普頓 白 艾可萊薩爾享年62歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾盧黑。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lucia De Ross于1270年出生在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾普倫普頓。她于大约1295年与Robert De Plumpton III在英国英格兰越可爾普倫普頓 白 艾可萊薩爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1332年在英国英格兰越可爾普倫普頓 白 艾可萊薩爾享年62岁，埋葬在英国英格兰越可爾盧黑。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ross
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

variants.name: Ruskin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Tauba, Uzi.
English: from the Middle English personal name Rosce (see Ross 4) + the diminutive suffix -kin.
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish (Ashkenazic) surname, for example   Raskin  .

variants.name: Ros
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.
Swedish: ornamental name from ros ‘rose’.

variants.name: Rose
meanings: English, Scottish, French, Walloon, Danish, and German: from the name of the flower, Middle English, Old French, Middle High German rose (from Latin rosa), in various applications. In part, it is a topographic name for someone who lived at a place where wild roses grew, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house bearing the sign of the rose. It is also found, especially in Europe, as a nickname for a man with a ‘rosy’ complexion (compare 4 below). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and similar-sounding names from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Rózsa (see Rozsa ), Slovak Róža and Czech Roza . Compare 6 below and French Larose 2.
English: from the Middle English female personal name Rohese, Roese, later Rose, Royse (ancient Germanic Hrodohaidis, Rothaid, composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + haid(is) ‘kind, sort’).
English and Scottish: variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Rossell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Ros(c)el, a diminutive of Rosce (see Ross ). Compare the double diminutive Roscelin, with which Ro(s)cel or Ros(s)el was probably freely interchangeable.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rosel in Calvados.
English: variant of   Russell  , with which the name in 1 above was easily confused.

variants.name: Rusling
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): patronymic from the Middle English personal name Rocelin (Old French Roscelin, Rocelin, ancient Germanic Ruozelin), a double diminutive of Rozo (see Ross ).

variants.name: Rohs
meanings: German: variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Rosse
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Dirk, Joris.
German: topographic name from Middle High German rœsse ‘kiln’, in particular one used for drying hemp and flax or for burning lime.
English and Scottish (Lanarkshire): variant of   Ross  .

nameMeanings.name: Lucia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Feminine form of the old Roman given name Lucius, which is probably a derivative of Latin lux ‘light’. The girl's name is common in Italy and elsewhere, and is found as a learned, Latinate doublet of Lucy in England. St Lucia of Syracuse, who was martyred in 304 , was a very popular saint in the Middle Ages; she is often represented in medieval art as blinded and with her eyes on a platter, but the tradition that she had her eyes put out is probably based on nothing more than the association between light and eyes.

en.lifeSummary: Lucia De Ross was born in 1270, in Plumpton, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom. She married Robert De Plumpton III about 1295, in Plumpton by Ecclesall, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1332, in Plumpton by Ecclesall, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 62, and was buried in Ryther, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Lucia De Rossは1270年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアプルンプトンで生まれた。約1295年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアプルンプトン ビー エククレサルでRobert De Plumpton IIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1332年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアプルンプトン ビー エククレサルで62歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアリスエルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lucia De Ross(은)는 1270년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 플럼프턴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1295년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 플럼프턴 비 치레사에서 Robert De Plumpton III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1332년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 플럼프턴 비 치레사에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 리터에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Lucia De Ross родилась в 1270 г. в местности плумптон, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert De Plumpton III около 1295 г. в местности плумптон би экклесалл, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1332 г. в местности плумптон би экклесалл, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности ритир, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.
